_provenance.what: Empirical capture of the `rewind_files` control request round-trip (the code-restore dimension of terminal /rewind).
_provenance.method: Direct stream-json driver against real claude (NOT through tugcode — tugcode's rewind_files IPC path lands in #step-7). Session spawned with CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_SDK_FILE_CHECKPOINTING=true, --permission-mode bypassPermissions; turn 1 had claude Write a file (creating a checkpoint), then the two control requests below were sent on stdin.

placeholders.{{cwd}}: absolute working directory of the session (machine-specific; normalized here)

notes: rewind_files is a CLIENT → claude control request (same direction as interrupt/set_model), NOT the claude→client control_request_forward used for tool gates.
Requires file checkpointing enabled; otherwise the response is {canRewind:false, error:"File rewinding is not enabled."}.
A message with no checkpoint yields {canRewind:false, error:"No file checkpoint found ..."} — this is the 'how far back you can restore' limit.
dry_run:true returns the diff stats WITHOUT applying (drives the picker's per-row +N -M and the 'No code changes' rows); dry_run:false applies the restore (the file was actually reverted/removed).

captures.label: dry_run — preview the restore (picker diff stats)
apply — perform the restore (file reverted on disk)
captures.side_effect: note.txt (created in turn 1) was deleted on disk — the restore genuinely reverts file edits.
